description:
You are acting as the SRE on‐call for a small Kubernetes cluster.  
    In the working directory you will find a predefined set of YAML
    manifests located in  
      /home/user/manifests/  

        deployment.yaml  
        service.yaml  
        configmap.yaml  

    Your assignment:

    1. Update the container image in deployment.yaml  
       • Inside the single container spec, change  
         image: nginx:1.19  
         to  
         image: nginx:1.20  

    2. Produce a concise inventory of the three manifests.  
       • For every YAML file, extract the values of the “kind” field and
         the “metadata.name” field.  
       • Create a CSV file at  
         /home/user/output/manifest_summary.csv  
         with the following **exact** structure (comma-separated, no
         extra spaces):  

            filename,kind,name  
            deployment.yaml,Deployment,webserver  
            service.yaml,Service,web-svc  
            configmap.yaml,ConfigMap,web-config  

         The header line must be present and the order of the data rows
         must be exactly as shown above.

    3. Generate a textual log confirming the image update.  
       • Write a single line with **no leading/trailing spaces** to  
         /home/user/output/update.log  
         The line must read (case-sensitive):  

            UPDATED: deployment.yaml image nginx:1.19 -> nginx:1.20  

    Implementation constraints and tips:

    • Complete the entire task using standard GNU utilities
      (awk, sed, echo, mkdir, etc.).  
    • Do not hard-code absolute paths in the CSV content; only the
      filenames (deployment.yaml, service.yaml, configmap.yaml) should
      appear as shown above.  
    • Feel free to create /home/user/output/ if it does not exist.  
    • No root privileges are available; work entirely as the normal user.  
    • The automated grader will verify:  
        – deployment.yaml now contains “image: nginx:1.20” (and **no**
          “nginx:1.19”).  
        – /home/user/output/manifest_summary.csv exists and its contents
          match the exact 4-line block shown above, **character for
          character**.  
        – /home/user/output/update.log exists and contains exactly the
          single line specified above.  

    Use as few commands as you like, but relying solely on awk and/or sed
    is required for the in-file edit and for constructing the CSV.
truth:
Initial file contents (already present when the task starts): /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml ------------------------------------------------------------ apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: webserver spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: web template: metadata: labels: app: web spec: containers: - name: nginx image: nginx:1.19 ports: - containerPort: 80 ------------------------------------------------------------ /home/user/manifests/service.yaml ------------------------------------------------------------ apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: web-svc spec: selector: app: web ports: - port: 80 targetPort: 80 ------------------------------------------------------------ /home/user/manifests/configmap.yaml ------------------------------------------------------------ apiVersion: v1 kind: ConfigMap metadata: name: web-config data: index.html: |

Hello World!

------------------------------------------------------------ Expected final state after the agent completes the task: 1. /home/user/manifests/deployment.yaml • Contains “image: nginx:1.20” and no occurrence of “nginx:1.19”. 2. /home/user/output/manifest_summary.csv (chmod 644) ------------------------------------------------------------ filename,kind,name deployment.yaml,Deployment,webserver service.yaml,Service,web-svc configmap.yaml,ConfigMap,web-config ------------------------------------------------------------ 3. /home/user/output/update.log (chmod 644) ------------------------------------------------------------ UPDATED: deployment.yaml image nginx:1.19 -> nginx:1.20 ------------------------------------------------------------
